summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the service with a prayer for God to bring a radical change to the congregation. He began by reading from Luke 24:46-47, highlighting the phrase 'a radical change of thought and life,' emphasizing that a genuine encounter with the Holy Spirit fundamentally transforms how we think and live. He declared that modern Christianity often feels lethargic and comfortable, contrasting it with the radical call of Jesus. The sermon, titled 'Do Something Radical,' was a passionate call to move beyond a mediocre, half-hearted faith. Pastor Travis outlined four radical actions: first, to truly 'follow me' as Jesus commanded, which means imitating Christ and dying to self daily. Second, to 'seek him first' in all areas, including our finances, by honoring God with our first fruits and tithes, trusting Him as our true provider. Third, to 'become a fisher of men,' using our everyday platforms—jobs, neighborhoods, relationships—to share the love of Jesus with others. Finally, he urged the church to 'be bold and unashamed' of the Gospel in a culture that often silences Christian witness. He closed by reminding everyone that they are the light of the world, called to shine for Jesus from their first step of faith to their last, and gave an invitation for salvation.
mainPoints: Following Jesus requires a radical, daily decision to deny self and imitate His life, not just a comfortable affiliation.
Putting God first in every area, especially our finances through tithing, is a foundational act of radical faith and trust.
Every believer is a minister and missionary, called to be bold and unashamed in sharing the Gospel within their unique sphere of influence.

versesMentioned.text: This is what the scriptures said: that the promised anointed one should suffer and rise from the dead on the third day, that in his name a radical change of thought and life should be preached, and that in his name the forgiveness of sins should be preached, beginning in Jerusalem and extending to all nations.
Follow God’s example, therefore, as dearly loved children

Then he said to them all: 'Whoever wants to be my disciple must deny themselves and take up their cross daily and follow me. For whoever wants to save their life will lose it, but whoever loses their life for me will save it. What good is it for someone to gain the whole world, and yet lose or forfeit their very self?'
But seek first his kingdom and his righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.
You shall have no other gods before me.

Bring the whole tithe into the storehouse, that there may be food in my house. Test me in this,' says the LORD Almighty, 'and see if I will not throw open the floodgates of heaven and pour out so much blessing that there will not be room enough to store it.'
Honor the LORD with your wealth, with the firstfruits of all your crops; then your barns will be filled to overflowing, and your vats will brim over with new wine.
'Come, follow me,' Jesus said, 'and I will send you out to fish for people.' At once they left their nets and followed him.

How, then, can they call on the one they have not believed in? And how can they believe in the one of whom they have not heard? And how can they hear without someone preaching to them? And how can anyone preach unless they are sent? As it is written: 'How beautiful are the feet of those who bring good news!'

Therefore go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit, and teaching them to obey everything I have commanded you. And surely I am with you always, to the very end of the age.
For I am not ashamed of the gospel, because it is the power of God that brings salvation to everyone who believes: first to the Jew, then to the Gentile.
so that you may become blameless and pure, 'children of God without fault in a warped and crooked generation.' Then you will shine among them like stars in the sky

You are the light of the world. A town built on a hill cannot be hidden. Neither do people light a lamp and put it under a bowl. Instead they put it on its stand, and it gives light to everyone in the house. In the same way, let your light shine before others, that they may see your good deeds and glorify your Father in heaven.
For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him shall not perish but have eternal life.

Monday.scripture: Luke 9:23 - 'Then he said to them all: ‘Whoever wants to be my disciple must deny themselves and take up their cross daily and follow me.’'
Monday.action: Identify one personal desire or comfort you will consciously deny today to make more room for Jesus.
Monday.prayer: Lord, give me the strength to deny myself today. Help me to see my cross not as a burden, but as the path to true life in You.

Tuesday.scripture: Matthew 6:33 - 'But seek first his kingdom and his righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.'
Tuesday.action: Before checking your phone or starting your work, spend the first 10 minutes of your day in prayer and reading Scripture.
Tuesday.prayer: God, I choose to put You first today. Align my heart with Your kingdom and help me trust that You will provide all I need.

Wednesday.scripture: Malachi 3:10 - 'Bring the whole tithe into the storehouse... Test me in this,’ says the LORD Almighty.’
Wednesday.action: Review your finances. If you are not already, make a plan to give God the first 10% of your income as an act of worship and trust.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, I acknowledge that everything I have is from You. I choose to trust You with my finances. Open the windows of heaven as I step out in faith.

Thursday.scripture: Matthew 4:19 - '‘Come, follow me,’ Jesus said, ‘and I will send you out to fish for people.’'
Thursday.action: Pray for one person in your life who doesn't know Jesus, and look for a natural opportunity to express care or share your faith story.
Thursday.prayer: Jesus, make me a fisher of men. Give me Your heart for the lost and the courage to see my workplace and relationships as my mission field.

Friday.scripture: Romans 1:16 - 'For I am not ashamed of the gospel, because it is the power of God that brings salvation to everyone who believes.'
Friday.action: In a conversation today, find a way to mention something you're grateful to God for or how your faith helps you.
Friday.prayer: Holy Spirit, fill me with boldness. Let me be unashamed of the good news of Jesus, and let my words reflect Your power and love.

Saturday.scripture: Matthew 5:14 - 'You are the light of the world. A town built on a hill cannot be hidden.'
Saturday.action: Perform one intentional act of kindness for a neighbor or stranger, letting God's love shine through you without seeking credit.
Saturday.prayer: God, help me to shine Your light today. May my actions point others to You and bring glory to Your name in my community.
